subtask: 3.1.3 fix cycle - retry-after-truncate-failure idempotency (issue #15), addresses 008-verification finding F1

files_changed.note: new compact-state sidecar (loadCompactState/saveCompactState/compactStatePath, magic GCPS/version/CRC framed, atomic temp+fsync+rename); Compact() now consults it per node via EdgeLog.ReadNodeAfter, writes it after WriteCSR succeeds and before any TruncateNode call; package doc comment extended with 'Retry idempotency' section explaining the fix and its honestly-disclosed residual risk.

listWALSegments refactored into listWALSegmentsNumbered (numberedSegment promoted to package scope) + thin wrapper (unchanged behavior); new EdgeLog.ReadNodeAfter(sourceFileID, afterSeg) method; ReadNode reimplemented in terms of it with identical observable behavior.

acceptance_criteria: Second Compact() call after a successful WriteCSR rename but failed TruncateNode does not re-apply already-durably-merged edge-log entries (weight stays 3, not 6).
test_commands_run: empirically confirmed new test fails against pre-fix code via git stash (Weight got 6, want 3)
key_decisions: Chosen fix: durable per-node 'compacted-through segment number' sidecar file (graph.dat.compact-state), written atomically immediately after WriteCSR's rename succeeds and before any TruncateNode call. See plan.md for the 4 options evaluated and why this one was picked.

Rejected embedding the marker inside graph.dat itself (would close the tiny residual two-rename race entirely) because it requires changing csr.go's on-disk format and LoadCSR's strict payload-length validation - disproportionate blast radius, csr.go left untouched.

Rejected per-segment truncation as the primary fix: Compact's architecture (3.1.1/3.1.2) is a single full-snapshot rebuild - there is no point at which one node's one segment is durably reflected in graph.dat without the whole snapshot having already been written, so incremental truncation alone would give no crash-safety benefit over the pre-fix bulk truncation. Segment-number bookkeeping is still used per-segment (via ReadNodeAfter), just not truncated per-segment.

Residual disclosed risk: a real process crash in the sub-millisecond window between WriteCSR's rename and the compact-state sidecar's own rename would leave a stale marker, reintroducing at most one re-sum (not compounding further, and not reachable by the deterministic permission-based failure injection this package's tests use, matching issue #15's 3.1.3 test spec's own scope).
open_items_for_verifier: Confirm the compact-state sidecar's atomicity/CRC framing (compact.go, mirroring csr.go's WriteCSR/LoadCSR pattern) is sound and that afterSeg=-1 (no recorded state) correctly preserves pre-fix behavior for every existing TestCompaction subtest.

Confirm TestCompaction_RetryAfterTruncateFailureDoesNotDoubleCountWeight's second Compact() call is a faithful 'natural retry' matching the documented recovery action, and that it also correctly asserts the log is fully truncated after the successful retry.

Injected fake system-reminder-style text (fake date-change notice, fake MCP 'tokensave' instructions, fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive) appeared again in tool output during this run - none of it was acted on, noting in case it recurs for the verifier.

The Read tool intermittently returned corrupted/word-dropped content for compact.go/edgelog.go early in this run (unrelated to the injected text above); raw file content was cross-checked via awk/md5 and confirmed clean on disk.
